extraction.one_line_summary: MCCARTHY Jay won the sprint finish with a perfectly timed kick, half a bike length ahead of MÜHLBERGER Gregor, while MATTHEWS Michael disappeared.
tactical_patterns: MCCARTHY Jay's punchy kick sprint unsuited to pure sprinters; MATTHEWS Michael completely out of position suggests transition stages expose specialists.
ALAPHILIPPE Julian sprinted hard for bonus seconds despite no win chance, revealing GC time gaps tight enough for aggressive accumulation.
KWIATKOWSKI Michał did extensive leadout work then contested sprint himself, indicating tactical flexibility when team sprinter absent or struggling.

rider_signals.MCCARTHY Jay: positive: demonstrated superior punch-sprint timing on transition finishes, half-bike win suggests explosive acceleration advantage over fastmen on rolling terrain
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: completely absent from sprint finish despite being pre-race favorite, signals weakness on punchy rollers or positioning collapse
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: actively contested bonus seconds despite no win threat, shows GC aggression and confidence in time trial capability
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: neutral: performed leadout duties then sprinted competitively, tactical utility player role but lacked finishing punch against specialists
rider_signals.MÜHLBERGER Gregor: positive: second place after extended leadout work shows unusual sprint capacity for domestique role, possible breakaway threat
